pu_verbatim.en:
ADJECTIVE all; abundant, countless, bountiful, every, plentiful
NOUN abundance, everything, life, universe
NUMBER 100
pu_verbatim.fr:
ADJECTIF tous, abondants, innombrables
NOM tout, abondance, la vie, l'univers
NOMBRE 100
pu_verbatim.de:
ADJEKTIV alle, jede/r/s; im Überfluss, zahllos, reichlich

ADJEKTIVO ĉiuj; abunda, nenombrebla, ĉiu
SUBSTANTIVO abundo, ĉio, vivo, universo

translations.commentary:
"ali" is an alternative to "ale", coined in a poll in 2002 in order to avoid confusion with "ala". However, the usage of "ale" over "ali" remains more common.
The majority of speakers report not understanding the "abundant, bountiful, plentiful" meaning, at least out of context.
"20, 120" were non-pu meanings previously in use in the community alongside 100; they are virtually unheard of nowadays.
translations.definition: all, every, everything, entirety, universe; (number) one hundred

translations.commentary: "ali" is an alternative to "ale", coined in a poll in 2002 in order to avoid confusion with "ala". However, the usage of "ale" over "ali" has been more common since at least the release of pu. Generally not considered a word in its own right.

konwe.author_verbatim:
KONWE
Conway's game of life -> konwe
emergent complex autonomous behaviors are a big part of Conway's game of life, which I think matches my target definition very well
author: jan Emalan

translations.commentary:
Conway refers to John Horton Conway, creator of the cellular automaton Game of Life.
konwe's intended meaning extends beyond just "living" and "life." It can be used to describe rocks or rivers or other nonliving things

translations.commentary: In common usage (both before and after pu), "lukin" and "oko" are separate words, each with their own meaning. In pu, they are considered synonyms. Those who consider them synonyms typically don't use "oko" at all, preferring "lukin" in all circumstances. The usage of "alasa" as a preverb meaning "try to" is more common than "lukin". In pu, only "lukin" has this meaning. A very common beginner mistake is treating "lukin" as if it means "appearance", due to the mistakenly equating the "visual" meaning as in "pona lukin (visually good)" to mean "looks good".

sutopatikuna.author_verbatim: Dear Sonja, That's a great idea, but if you do so, you need also to add a special word forthe Platypus (Ornithorhynchus anatinus). I would suggest the word "sutopatikuna"from the croatian word Èudnovati kljuna¹. Bonega ideo, sed se vi farus tion, vi devus ankaux aldoni apartan vorton porla Ornitorinko (Ornithorhynchus anatinus). Mi proponus la vorton "sutopatikuna"prenita de la kroatan vorton Èudnovati kljuna¹.:P
author: Alexandre Baudry
